mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate (ZIRP) indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which exerts downward pressure on long-term interest rates by signaling a commitment to low borrowing costs, thus leading to a negative direction for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS near zero lower bound in 2012
failure_condition: 10-year U.S. Treasury yields produce positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals a rapidly strengthening labor market, which increases expectations for future economic growth and inflation, thereby pushing the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield in a positive direction over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 7.7% in Nov 2012, showing recovery
failure_condition: 10-year U.S. Treasury yields produce negative 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE falling by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates a robust and broad-based recovery in consumer and investment demand, which raises expectations for future economic activity and inflation, leading to a positive direction for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year U.S. Treasury yields produce negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances when both HOUST > 850k and RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM are met.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment, any positive month-over-month increase in the Consumer Price Index signals improving economic health and reduces deflationary concerns, prompting investors to demand higher compensation for inflation risk, thus pushing the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield in a positive direction over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year U.S. Treasury yields produce negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL growth > 0.1% MoM, specifically when CPIAUCSL YoY is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in industrial production reflects a healthy and expanding manufacturing sector, indicating robust economic activity and increasing demand for capital, which leads to a positive direction for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 97.509 in Nov 2012, indicating recovery
regime_hint: Any regime with positive industrial growth
failure_condition: 10-year U.S. Treasury yields produce negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong job creation and a tightening labor market, which accelerates expectations for future economic growth and potential monetary policy tightening, leading to a positive direction for the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000 persons month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year U.S. Treasury yields produce negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite PAYEMS increasing by more than 150,000 persons.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signifies an expanding economy, which naturally increases the demand for capital and raises inflation expectations, thereby driving the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield in a positive direction over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year U.S. Treasury yields produce negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) environment, a sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals that the economy is approaching full employment, increasing market expectations for an eventual shift in Federal Reserve policy towards tightening, which pushes the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield in a positive direction over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.5% AND FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS near zero, UNRATE falling in 2012
failure_condition: 10-year U.S. Treasury yields produce negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances when UNRATE falls below 7.5% and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.
